name: Garden of Eden
description: The Garden of Eden, also known as Gan Eden, is the idyllic paradise depicted in the Book of Genesis, representing humanity's original state of innocence and harmony with God and nature. It is described as a lush garden planted by God, filled with fruit-bearing trees, including the Tree of Life and the Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil. Adam and Eve, the first humans, dwelled in this garden, living in perfect communion with God and lacking any knowledge of good and evil, suffering, or death. The garden's significance lies in its representation of a perfect world, a lost paradise, and the origin of sin and suffering in human existence. The narrative of the Garden of Eden explores themes of temptation, disobedience, free will, and the consequences of choosing knowledge over obedience. Its expulsion marks the beginning of human history and the separation of humanity from God's direct presence. The story continues to resonate as a foundational myth shaping theological and philosophical perspectives on human nature, morality, and the relationship between humanity and the divine.
subtitle: Paradise, Innocence, and the Fall

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Adam and Eve
keyMyths.description: God creates Adam from dust and Eve from Adam's rib, placing them in the Garden of Eden to cultivate it. They are given dominion over all living creatures and are instructed to enjoy all the fruits of the garden except for the fruit of the Tree of Knowledge of Good and Evil.

keyMyths.title: The Fall from Grace
keyMyths.description: A serpent tempts Eve to eat the forbidden fruit, promising her and Adam knowledge and god-like status. Eve succumbs to temptation and shares the fruit with Adam, leading to their expulsion from the Garden and the introduction of sin and mortality into the world.

sources.author: Moses
sources.description: The primary source for the Garden of Eden narrative.
sources.text: The primary source for the Garden of Eden narrative.

sources.author: Various Rabbis
sources.description: A compilation of Rabbinic interpretations of the Hebrew Bible, offering insights into the Garden of Eden.
